title: US-001: Filter process list by name
description:
As a developer, I want to pass --filter=<name> so that only processes whose names contain the given substring are shown.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] sysinfo --filter=nginx shows only processes whose name contains "nginx" (case-insensitive)
- [ ] If no processes match, the process section shows an empty list with a "No matching processes" message
- [ ] Other sections (CPU, memory, disk) are unaffected by --filter
- [ ] Invalid or empty --filter value shows a helpful error message
- [ ] python -m pytest passes

title: Filtering and Sorting for sysinfo CLI
description: Add --filter and --sort=mem flags to the sysinfo CLI process list for developer workflow.

title: US-002: Sort process list by memory usage
description:
As a developer, I want to pass --sort=mem so that the process list is ordered by memory usage from highest to lowest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] sysinfo --sort=mem outputs processes in descending order of memory usage
- [ ] Without --sort, output preserves existing default ordering
- [ ] Passing an unrecognized sort key (e.g., --sort=foo) shows a clear error with valid options listed
- [ ] python -m pytest passes

title: US-003: Combine filter and sort flags
description:
As a developer, I want to use --filter and --sort=mem together so I can find the most memory-hungry instances of a specific process.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] sysinfo --filter=python --sort=mem returns only Python processes, sorted by memory descending
- [ ] The combination produces correct results (filter applied first, then sort)
- [ ] Both flags are independently optional; either can be used alone or together
- [ ] python -m pytest passes
